text: The video shows the in situ colorectal anastomosis after resection of rectal cancer. Fluorescence endoscopy is used to confirm perfusion adequacy, with indocyanine green administered via peripheral cannula. A near-infrared laparoscope views the anastomosis from within through a proctoscope, while a second laparoscope observes from the abdominal side.

The video shows excellent perfusion of the colorectal anastomosis, highlighted by fluorescence both proximal and distal to the circular stapled anastomosis. The serosal aspect of the bowel also demonstrates excellent perfusion, as observed through the second laparoscopic system.
More information on near-infrared laparoscopy can be found elsewhere in this YouTube channel and in the special supplement of colorectal disease.
